Aegyo Theater: Korean Television: Come get a glimpse of Korean culture all while watching some of the funniest, nerdiest and most clever TV you will see! We will have some of South Korea's most popular TV shows for your perusal and even provide a quick reference sheet of commonly used Korean words. Come join the fun!
Apprentice Movie Night: A line-up of family-friendly movies for our Apprentices to enjoy!
Build Your Own Bloody Mary: All the spices and fixin's you could ask for! Come make your own Bloody Mary with or without advice from your host Casey.
Cabaret 101: Join in on some cabaret dance basics with a holiday twist! Dance shoes or socks are encouraged, but barefoot is fine, too!
Cantina Counter Service: We'll be serving so you can have some refreshments while enjoying the other events!
Coffee Service: Your morning pick-me-up. Coffee with a variety of creamer and sweetener options will be available. A keurig is available if you'd like to bring your own special pods. 21+ may come inside Mayfield if you'd like some additions to your coffee.
Color Wars!:
Get ready for a festive showdown where every holiday gets its chance to shine! In this year's Color Wars, teams will compete through a series of hilarious, creative, and occasionally ridiculous holiday-themed challenges inspired by celebrations from around the world. Have a team ready? Excited to see you there. No team? No Problem, come join in the fun anyway and we will find a team to adopt you.

Teams can even sign up as the event starts, so don't feel like you can't join-in last minute.
Con In Review: We consider this convention to be a community event. Your input is not only desired but needed if we are to make POC into the best convention it can be. Please join us as we discuss Play On Con 2026 and plan for POC 20 in 2027.
Costume Contest:
Come one, come all, to the biggest costume gala of the weekend. Awards go to "Best in Show", "Most Creative", and "Fan Favorite". Prizes will also be awarded for other categories that will be announced at the event ("Best Group," "Best Anime Character," etc.). Be there in your best and strut your stuff for this on Saturday night!

All costumes entered in any POC Programming contest must be PG-13 or lower. Family show, folks! Please save the grown-up costumes for the 21+ party area.
Craft & Chill: Bring your supplies for an hour of leisurely group crafting (knitting, crochet, cross stitch, spinning, etc) and with a side of coloring. This is a grown-up oriented event where you can chill and meet other crafting enthusiasts. If you're into coloring, we even have some supplies you can borrow!
Crochet Craft Session: If you want some time to share your knits and pearls, have we got the time for you! Come join in as we share our creations and crochet together.
Dinner Theater: Holiday Trivia: Are you the kind of person who loves Flag Day? Tell your friends about Festivus? Just really good at random knowledge? Come play pub style trivia with a festive flair! Teams will compete in two rounds of ten questions each with more excitement than Christmas morning!
Egg Nog Class & Tasting: Join the Reverend for a class on how to make boozy egg nog from scratch…and age it. Participants will sample a fresh preparation plus one aged for a month, and a third aged with extra prep before presentation. Perfect for a hot summer day!
Fantasy Map Making Workshop: Create your own magical map using stamps, specialty papers, inks, textures, and mixed-media tools. Participants will design fantasy lands filled with winding rivers, hidden forests, mountain kingdoms, mysterious ruins, and secret paths. Perfect for storytellers, artists, D&D players, and anyone who wants to build a world on paper, no cartography experience required.
Holiday Mixer - ALL AGES!: Kick off the convention by meeting new friends and reconnecting with familiar faces at our Holiday Mixer! Break the ice with fun getting-to-know-you games, take part in our optional "Drinking Around the World" challenge featuring holiday-inspired beverages (with plenty of alcohol-free options available), and share your favorite memories from PlayOnCons past. Whether it's your first year or you're a longtime camper, this is the perfect way to celebrate, laugh, and start the weekend together.
Holiday TV Episodes: We love a good holiday episode in our favorite TV shows! Come see some of our favorites and feel the joy of a special holiday episode!
How To: Tie Dye: Tie Dye is a great way to make custom clothing without breaking the bank. Learn how to turn fabric into a testament to love and peace! This event includes our Apprentices. BYOT, but a few shirts will be available at the event.
Iron Bartender: Superior Mixmasters battle neophyte challengers in number one challenge to the death! Judges demand drinks with secret ingredient, and bartenders do as they are told. Watch as Cocktail Stadium becomes mighty arena of conflict!
Jackbox Party (Wifi Permitting): Join us for some Jackbox Party games in the Theater! We're going to experiment whether the wifi can support it! Bring a tablet or phone!
Jam Session: Join in an open jam in the Guthrie Lodge. We have a wide variety of instruments that might be stopping in, including ukuleles, guitars, flutes, and even a hurdy gurdy! Be sure to bring some water. All skill levels are welcome to jam out some tunes!
Karaoke: We will be featuring karaoke in various places around the show this year. We have it in the theater on Thursday Night, by the pool on Friday and Saturday, and some Adults Only Karoke in Lower Mayfield on Saturday night. Plus we have…
Karaoke Battle: Think you've got the pipes to win it all? Step up to the mic for our Karaoke Battle, where singers go head to head in a bracket style showdown until one champion is crowned.
Kickoff Pool Party: It's a pooltime paradise to start off the weekend! Come jump in the pool and enjoy good tunes!
Leveling Up: Kids, Games, and Growing Mi: Join therapist Christine Penguino, LCSW, and Dr. Sandy Whetmore, PhD in Early Childhood Education, for a discussion on the positive impact of gaming on children's development. From tabletop adventures to video games, we'll explore how games can build creativity, problem-solving, emotional resilience, social connection, and family bonding. Whether you're a parent, educator, gamer, or just curious about why kids love rolling dice and pressing start, this panel will separate the "screen time goblins" from the real-world benefits hiding in the treasure chest.
Make a Holiday Card: Spread joy and encouragement this POC by joining our Apprentice Track Holiday Card Activity! Participants will create fun, handmade holiday cards filled with kind messages, drawings, and well wishes for whomever you want. All card-making supplies will be provided—just bring your creativity and a heart for encouraging others.
Malevolent Karaoke: Make your loved ones and friends sing songs they hate for fun and fabulous prizes! Two-person teams are required, and you'll score higher if your partner really knows which songs make you squirm. We'll pair up any single players, though, so feel free to come alone! Judges will assign scores in Malevolence, Performance, and Audience Reaction.
Morning Cartoons: Start your day off with the breakfast of champions… CARTOONS!
Music: Professor Shyguy: Come join Professor Shyguy for a flurry of chiptune rock. With his anthems to everything from The Guild to Portal, this show is destined to appeal to all your nerdy bits.
Music: Sparkling Noise: Sparkling Noise returns with an (almost) all-new Holiday Show that takes you on a journey through the year! Jon, Art, Kat, Kate, Logan, Corey, and Buehler are back, serving up hot holiday jams and a couple of Play On classics to make your convention merry and bright.
Open Cantina: Come chill out in Lower Mayfield with us! We'll be serving and have the area open for people to relax. There maybe classes going on as well.
Perler Bead Crafts: Perler beads are small, colorful plastic beads that can be arranged on a pegboard and fused together with heat to create permanent designs. Come make your own creations with us!
Pictures with Santa:
It's an Olan Mills Miracle!

"The line in this mall isn't TOO long. Maybe this year he won't smell quite as bad as last year's Santa. Wait, that's him? Isn't he a little… I dunno… skinny to be Santa? That pillow isn't doing him justice. Maybe this picture will be good for the holiday card we're sending out. Did you make sure all the kids knew how to ask for their present? Wait, who's THAT?!?!"

Wes and Dan invite you to come get Polaroid pictures with Santa in the Lodge!
POC: Holiday Variety Show:
A special showcase of the incredible vocal and performance talents of our con staff and casted attendees. This is not your average talent show—this is a vibrant, casted production that shines a spotlight on the voices, stories, and creativity of the people within our community.

From powerful solos to dynamic group numbers, you'll experience a range of musical styles, theatrical moments, and pure joy on stage. Whether you're cast in the show, cheering from the audience or clapping along to the beat, this is a show you won't want to miss!
Poolside Karaoke: Croon poolside for some afternoon delight… full karaoke goodness. Sing loud, sing proud, and make everyone at the 4-H know you're there!
Presidential Nominations & Table Flippin:
Let your rage be known! Let out a primal yawp and flip the table -- greatest distance wins. Bonus prizes may be awarded for style, grace, or pure rage.

Presidential Nominations will take place at the beginning of this event.
Rocky Horror Picture Show: The Rocky Horror Picture Show with audience partici...pation! Costumes and props are encouraged, though we'll restrict anything that's messy (so no food or anything that can't be cleaned up within a couple of minutes). We'll have a quick 5-10 minute intro at the beginning, then show the movie.
Star Wars Holiday Special:
Look. We know it's bad. It's REALLY bad. But is it so bad it's good? Probably not. Still, it was a major inspiration for this year's theme, so we ALL get to experience it!

We're sorry…. maybe.
Star Wars Trivia (Holiday Special & More: Come join host Tony Black (aka Ohio Tony) for Star Wars (Holiday Special and more) Trivia! Compete in teams or individually to win a fabulous prize. Plus, there will be inflatable lightsabers! Be warned, this will not be easy.
Tis the Season to Make Punch: Nothing says holiday gathering quite like a punch bowl. Join Lisa for a spirited tour through punch's 400-year history, from East India Company sailors to George Washington's Christmas bowl to your next holiday party. Learn the legendary five-element formula, explore many classics, and walk away with recipes that will make you the most dangerous host of the season.
Um, Actually: "Um, Actually" is the game where geeks correct other geeks. The host will read several fandom facts aloud, with each fact having an incorrect piece of information. The player who can best correct the statement must buzz in and make the correction, but don't forget to precede your answer with "Um, Actually."
Valentine's Speed Meeting: It's like Speed Dating, but for new friends! Come join a circle of fellow POCsters and meet every one of them before the session ends!
Welcome Wagon: Now to Play On Con? We've all been there! Come join a panel of Play On Con regulars as they share their advice for making the most of the weekend and answer your questions about what to expect.
White Elephant Cocktail: Join your host Jared for White Elephant or Dirty Santa (depending on who you ask). Come choose a cocktail card, but be careful, you might not get to keep it for long! He will jam out the cocktails as they are picked. No gifts please, just bring yourself!
Archery: Whether you imagine yourself as Robin Hood or Hawkeye, you owe yourself a chance to see how you stack up!
Beer Croquet: Join The Reverend for a round of BYOB croquet! One hand MUST be distracted with a beverage to play. Seersucker suits are appropriate. 21+ Only!
Boat On!: Take a leisurely trip afloat on Lay Lake. Pick from Canoes, Kayaks and Paddleboards! Canoeing sessions are limited to 30 people.
Climbing Wall: The day never begins until you cling precariously to a cliff face. This activity should be considered a metaphor.
Cooler Yacht-Zee: We have two sets of large outdoor dice and have a drinking game of Yahtzee (or 2) with them! We will bring a cooler of drinks and you can also byob. Ideally right after beer croquet.
Easter Egg Hunt:
Get ready for a fun-filled Easter adventure designed especially for our Apprentice Track participants! Join us at the miniature golf course for an exciting Easter Egg Hunt where you'll search for colorful eggs hidden throughout the course, enjoy the festive atmosphere, and connect with friends along the way.

Bring your Easter basket, wear comfortable shoes, and get ready for a time of laughter, teamwork, and friendly competition. Keep an eye out for special prize eggs hidden among the course—they could lead to extra surprises!
Nature Run/Walk: Start your day with a morning run or a leisurely walk. Runners can stick to the roads while the hikers hit the trails! What will we find? No bears, plz.
Yule Fire: Tis the Season to relax by the fire! Come join us as we bask in some warm flames and warm hearts.
Open Gaming:
With dozens of open tables and our library of hundreds of unique board and card games, you can fill your weekend with as much gaming as you could ever want! Open Gaming will be open from 10am till 2am each day of the convention, starting at 4pm on Thursday and ending at 2pm on Sunday.

Features of the Open Gaming room include:
- A massive space with late night open board and card gaming
- Stacks of games available for check-out to all attendees
- Friendly library staff who love teaching new games
- Opportunities to learn brand new releases as well as old classics
- "Play to Win" games are available so you can build your collection

Don't know anyone? New to boardgaming? No problem! Ask our library team what they recommend!! These dedicated gamers will be on hand to welcome newcomers, get people into games, and teach anything you might want to learn.

Come join us and get your game on!
Artemis: Bridge Simulator:
Ever dreamed of being a crew member on the Enterprise, fighting against a relentless horde of enemy ships to save the galaxy?

The Artemis Spaceship Bridge Simulator brings that experience to life. It is a multiplayer, cooperative, role-based game that allows players to command their very own starship. The odds are stacked against you, of course, so the crew must work together to manage weapons, shields, engines, power systems, and strategic information.

Each session of Artemis lasts approximately 45 minutes and accommodates a crew of six players. Each player will choose a completely unique role: Captain, Navigation, Tactical, Engineering, Science, or Communications.

Whether you want to bring a pre-assembled team or join a pick-up crew, this is an amazing experience that you won't want to miss!

IMPORTANT NOTE: Each Artemis mission begins on the hour. Pick up a ticket for your desired crew position and time-slot at the Open Gaming station, and then report to the Artemis Bridge at the assigned time.
Blood on the Clocktower:
A scream echoes through the sleepy town of Ravenswood Bluff. The townsfolk rush to investigate and find the beloved local storyteller has been murdered, their body hanging limp from the clocktower. As blood drips onto the cobblestones below, a realization slowly dawns… a demon has been unleashed, killing by night and taking on human form by day. Can good find the demon in time? Or will evil overrun this once peaceful town?

Blood on the Clocktower is a game of murder and mystery, lies and logic, deduction and deception for five-to-twenty courageous players and one devious storyteller.

A social game played in person, seated in a circle, each player receives either a 'good' or 'evil' token with their unique character on it. Good players share information to solve the mystery whilst evil players lie about who they are and what they know. The good team wins if they can piece together their knowledge, trust each other, and execute the demon. The evil team wins if the demon can sow distrust, evade detection, and wipe out the village.
Board Game Tournaments:
POC 2026 will feature a variety of Board Game Tournaments and "Learn to Play" events!

Thursday: Magical Athlete
Friday: Looot!
Saturday: Castle Combo

New to any of our tournament games? No problem! Each of the tournaments have a "Learn to Play" session at the start of each tournament.
Carnival of Doom TTRPG: Carnival of Doom Come have a fun day at the carnival and play some games and win some prizes you may or may not know you want or need, they are to die for! We promise you'll have a killer good time! The best part is you never have to leave (even if you want to)! Ages 12+.
Goblin Quest TTRPG: Goblin Quest is a tabletop roleplaying game about slapstick violence, fatal ineptitude, and the greatest adventure of your life. Play five goblins each (in sequence, not parallel) and watch them meet hilarious ends while failing to achieve the most basic tasks. Will they survive the dangerous world of the Great Battle Camp and avoid the attentions of brutal orcs, murderous bugbears, mean-spirited hobgoblins and scary wizards? Probably not. But you'll have fun finding out! Ages 12+.
Johann Sebastian Joust: Johann Sebastian Joust is a local multiplayer no-graphics contact sport video game without video. The objective of the game is to be the last player standing, having protected their controller from being jostled. Competitors eliminate each other by causing a rapid motion in their opponent's controller. Up to 7 players hold PS Move motion controllers, which they must protect from other players. When the music (J.S. Bach's Brandenburg Concertos) plays slowly, the controllers are very sensitive to movement. When the music speeds up, the threshold becomes less strict, giving the players a small window to dash at their opponents. If your controller is ever moved beyond the allowable threshold, you're out! Try to jostle your opponents' controllers while protecting your own.
Just Dance!: Start your morning with song and dance! A hearty round of energetic expression… on a dance pad. Friday's session will be for everyone, and Saturday will host an Apprentice session! Touching grass is overrated!
Learn to Play: Riftbound: Riftbound is an official collectible trading card game developed by Riot Games, set in the League of Legends universe. Players command armies of iconic champions, cast spells, and use gear to conquer and hold battlefields in a race to earn 8 points and win the game.
Magic the Gathering: Jumpstart: Drop in and jam! 2 provided packs and another player is all you need to play! This event will be open to players of all skill levels, and help provided for beginners with no time commitment necessary.
Magic the Gathering: Secret of Strixhave: Join up for a traditional draft in the Magic Universe's newest and best selling set! Draft will kick off at (time) followed by deck building and a 3 round, best of three bracket.
Parseley Live Text Adventure:
Re-live the golden age of text-based adventure games!

In this cooperative group game, your host is the text parser, and what happens next is completely up to you. Use your cunning and ingenuity to survive the epic challenges, or face a horrific demise. Either way, the players win or lose as a team. In past years, POCsters have saved a derelict space station, braved the horrors of a haunted mansion, and survived a shootout in the Old West. What devious challenges await you this time?

New players are welcome -- even if you've never touched an adventure game before, you'll be able to "go east" and "open mailbox" in no time!
Times Past TTRPG: You wake up in the barracks just like every day (right?). You meet the eyes of your companions and say your good mornings as you always do (right?), but this time as you do you catch a flash of a memory of a feeling of fear, betrayal, confusion, and war. It ends almost before it begins, but it's still there in the back of your mind. You expect this day to be just like "every other day" as always just as you "remember" them being, except this morning a man runs in panicking and says "The village is under attack by monsters!" (Which never happens, right?). What happens next is up to you. Interested? Come play and find out where the group will take the story next! Ages 12+.
Ultimate Gamer:
Back after a multi year break, it's the con tradition of The Ultimate Gamer. Once a weekend long gauntlet, now place your score on the tryout game in Video Gaming before Saturday morning and then show up and compete in an 8 person winner take all battle for the ultimate crown (crown included)!

If there's more room for ham: Prove to your friends and family that you are indeed the Ultimate Gamer, at least for this year. Guaranteed to finish in less than the entire weekend!
Video Games:
Our consoles will be available for free play for most of the weekend, so come in and get your game on! Whether you enjoy the newest fighting games or want to kick it old-school, we've got something for you!

Scheduled Tournaments include: Nidhogg, Super Smash Brothers Ultimate, and Tetris.

Check the Schedule for more information!
